content.text: Fix applied in both places — default dict with the key in routes.py, and `| default(false)` filters in the template as defense-in-depth. Let me verify it works.

input.command:
PYTHONPATH=./src python3 -c "
from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
from code_indexer.server.app import app
client = TestClient(app)

# Login
resp = client.post('/auth/login', json={'username': 'admin', 'password': 'admin'})
token = resp.json()['access_token']

# Access config page with Bearer (simulates authenticated session)
resp = client.get('/admin/config', headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {token}'})
print(f'GET /admin/config: {resp.status_code}')
if resp.status_code >= 500:
    print(f'ERROR: {resp.text[:300]}')
elif resp.status_code == 200:
    # Check if pace_maker section is present
    if 'pace_maker' in resp.text:
        print('pace_maker section present in HTML')
    if 'Enforce Pacing-Only Mode' in resp.text:
        print('Enforce Pacing-Only Mode label present')
    print(f'HTML length: {len(resp.text)}')
" 2>&1 | grep -v "^2026\|INFO\|WARNING\|DEBUG"
